questions.q: What is the Samudra Manthan, as recounted in the Puranas?
options: The churning of the cosmic ocean
questions.explanation: Samudra Manthan means the churning of the ocean, the cosmic event narrated in the Bhagavata and Vishnu Puranas.

questions.q: In the Samudra Manthan, which mountain was used as the churning rod?
questions.explanation: According to the Puranas, Mount Mandara served as the churning rod in the Samudra Manthan.

questions.q: In the Samudra Manthan, which serpent was used as the churning rope?
questions.explanation: The serpent Vasuki served as the rope wound around Mount Mandara during the Samudra Manthan.

questions.q: Who worked together to churn the ocean in the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: The devas (gods) and asuras (anti-gods) cooperated to churn the cosmic ocean in the Samudra Manthan.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu supported Mount Mandara during the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: Vishnu took the form of Kurma, the tortoise, to support the sinking Mount Mandara during the churning.

questions.q: What divine nectar of immortality were the gods seeking through the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: The chief goal of the Samudra Manthan was to obtain amrita, the nectar of immortality.

questions.q: Which goddess of fortune emerged from the ocean during the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: The goddess Lakshmi arose from the churning ocean and chose Vishnu as her consort.

questions.q: Which deadly poison emerged early in the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: The terrible poison Halahala emerged from the churning ocean, threatening all creation.

questions.q: Which god drank the poison Halahala to save creation during the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: Shiva drank the Halahala poison to protect the worlds, holding it in his throat.

questions.q: In which body of water did the Samudra Manthan take place, according to the Puranas?
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe the churning taking place in the Kshira Sagara, the cosmic ocean of milk.

questions.q: Which divine physician emerged from the Samudra Manthan bearing the pot of amrita?
questions.explanation: Dhanvantari, regarded as the physician of the gods and patron of Ayurveda, rose from the ocean carrying the amrita.

questions.q: Which celestial wish-fulfilling cow emerged during the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: Kamadhenu, the celestial wish-granting cow, was among the treasures yielded by the churning.

questions.q: Because the asuras objected to holding the serpent's tail, where did the devas position themselves on Vasuki during the Samudra Manthan?
options: At the head, near the venom
At the coils in the middle
They did not hold the serpent
questions.explanation: When the asuras insisted on grasping the head, the devas held Vasuki's tail, a placement that ultimately favored them.

questions.q: Which white, multi-headed celestial elephant emerged during the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: Airavata, the white celestial elephant who became Indra's mount, arose from the churning.

questions.q: Which divine white horse is said to have emerged from the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: Uchchaihshravas, the seven-headed white horse, was among the treasures of the churning.

questions.q: What enchanting female form did Vishnu assume to distribute the amrita to the devas during the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: Vishnu took the bewitching form of Mohini to recover the amrita and ensure it reached the devas.

questions.q: Which asura disguised himself among the gods and drank some amrita before being beheaded during the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: The asura Svarbhanu drank the amrita and, though beheaded, became immortal as Rahu and Ketu.

questions.q: Which two luminaries exposed the disguised asura who stole amrita during the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: Surya (the Sun) and Chandra (the Moon) recognized and exposed the disguised asura to Vishnu.

questions.q: In the Puranas, where did Shiva hold the Halahala poison he drank during the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: Shiva held the poison in his throat, which turned blue, earning him the name Nilakantha.

questions.q: By what epithet is Shiva known for having held the poison in his throat during the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: Holding the blue-stained poison in his throat earned Shiva the name Nilakantha, the blue-throated one.

questions.q: Which celestial tree, said to fulfil wishes, emerged during the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: The Kalpavriksha, the wish-granting tree, was among the treasures churned from the ocean.

questions.q: According to the Bhagavata Purana, why did the devas initially seek the asuras' help for the Samudra Manthan?
options: The task was too great for the devas alone
questions.explanation: Weakened by a sage's curse, the devas were advised by Vishnu to make a temporary pact with the asuras for the immense task.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, whose curse left the devas weakened, prompting the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: The sage Durvasa cursed Indra, draining the devas of strength and fortune, which set the churning in motion.

questions.q: Which radiant gem, later worn by Vishnu upon his chest, emerged from the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: The Kaustubha gem arose from the churning of the ocean, and the Puranas relate that Vishnu took it to adorn his chest.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, how was Mount Mandara obtained for use as the churning rod in the Samudra Manthan?
options: It was conjured from the sea foam
It was a gift from Varuna
The devas and asuras uprooted it and carried it to the ocean
It descended from the heavens on its own
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe Mandara as a great mountain that the devas and asuras together uprooted and carried to the ocean to serve as the churning rod.

questions.q: Which bird is described in the Puranas as helping carry Mount Mandara for the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: Garuda, Vishnu's eagle mount, is said to have helped transport the immense Mount Mandara to the ocean.

questions.q: Which radiant celestial body is counted in several Puranic lists among the treasures that emerged from the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: Chandra, the moon, is counted among the radiant treasures said to have arisen from the churning of the ocean in several Puranic lists.

questions.q: Which goddess of wine or intoxication is said in some Puranic lists to have emerged during the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: Varuni, also called Sura, the goddess associated with wine, is named among the fourteen treasures in several Puranic lists.

questions.q: Traditionally, how many treasures, or ratnas, are said to have emerged from the Samudra Manthan?
questions.explanation: The churning is traditionally said to have yielded fourteen ratnas, or treasures, though lists vary slightly across texts.

questions.q: In which texts is the Samudra Manthan most prominently recounted?
questions.explanation: The Samudra Manthan is recounted prominently in the Bhagavata and Vishnu Puranas, among other Puranic and epic sources.
